State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 307 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662         322-5660         322-5901         322-6441         322-6444

February 23, 1983


Mr. Jeffrey G. Scott
Smith and Peltzer
751 E. Rancheros Drive
Marcos, CA 92069

Re: Your Request for Advice, Our
Advice No. A-83-014

Dear Mr. Scott:

Thank you for your request for advice on Government Code
Section 84308.1 (AB 1040). Your question is whether the
provisions of this section apply to the members of hospital
district boards or water district boards.

Section 84308(d) exempts "legislative bodies ... while
acting as a body or committee thereof" from the coverage of this
section. A commission or board qualifies as a "legislative
body" only if its entire membership is directly elected by the
voters to serve on that body. (2 Cal. Adm. Code Section
18438.1(a).) The term "voters" is defined in Election Code
Section 18.

In the case of water districts, if a board is directly
elected by "voters" who meet the definition provided in Election
Code Section 18, the board is exempt from the coverage of
Section 84308.1/ However, if the voters electing a water
district board do not meet this definition,3/ the board is not


1/ Hereinafter all statutory references are to the
Government Code, unless otherwise indicated.

2/ Examples are County Water Districts (Water Code
Sections 30000, et seq.); Irrigation Districts (Water Code
Sections 20500, et seq.); and Municipal Water Districts (Water
Code Section 71000, et seq.).

3/ Examples are California Water Districts (Water Code
Sections 34000, et seq.) and Levee Districts (Water Code
Sections 70000, et seq.).

Mr. Jeffrey G. Scott
February 23, 1983
Page Two


exempt and, to the extent that the board makes decisions on
permits, licenses, entitlements for use or engages is other
quasi-judicial decision-making,1/ the provisions of Section
84308 are applicable.

In the case of hospital districts, it appears that the
boards are all directly elected by voters who meet the
definition provided in Election Code Section 18. (See Health
and Safety Code Sections 82000, et seq.) Thus, Section 84308
does not apply.2/

I hope I have clarified this matter for you. If you have
any additional questions, please feel free to contact me at
916/322-5901.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
Janis Shank McLean
Counsel
Legal Division

JSM:km


_____________________
1/ See 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18438(c).

2/ If there are instances where this is not true, Section
84308 would apply.

SMITH & PELTZER
A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION
ATTORNEYS AT LAW
751 E. RANCHEROS DRIVE
RANCHEROS, CALIFORNIA 92069

VERNON A. PELTZER
THOMAS W. SMITH III
WESLEY W. PELTZER

AREA CODE 714
TELEPHONE: 744-7125

JEFFREY G. SCOTT

January 20, 1983


Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 "K" Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: Application of AB 1040 (Chap. 1049) to Special Districts

The application of AB 1040 (and implementing regulations) to
special districts such as Hospital Districts (Health & Safety
Code §32000 et. seq.), Water Districts (Water Code §30000 et.
seq., 71000 et. seq.) is unclear.

The directors of such public agencies are elected and function as
a legislative body, not a quasi-judicial body.

Even though such directors do control such "entitlement" as
Hospital Staff privileges and use of public water and sewerage
facilities by applicants, it is concluded that AB 1040 (and the
implementing regulations) do not apply to those directors [not
quasi-judicial and exempt under subsection "(d)"].

Please advise concerning the FPPC opinion on this matter.

SMITH & PELTZER

By ________________________
JEFFREY G. SCOTT

JGS/gat

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5660           322-5901          322-6441              327-6444


January 26, 1983


Jeffrey G. Scott
Smith & Peltzer
A Professional Corporation
741 E. Rancheros Drive
San Marcos, CA 92069

Re: A-83-014

Dear Mr. Scott,

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political
Reform Act has been referred to Robert Leidigh, an attorney
in the Legal Division of the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice
request, you may contact this attorney directly at
(916) 322-5901.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly.
Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex
legal questions, or unless more information is needed to
answer your request, you should expect a response within 21
working days.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
Barbara A. Milman
Chief
Legal Division

BAM:km
Enclosure

sections.question: Your question is whether the provisions of this section apply to the members of hospital district boards or water district boards.
sections.conclusion: In the case of water districts, if a board is directly elected by "voters" who meet the definition provided in Election Code Section 18, the board is exempt from the coverage of Section 84308. However, if the voters electing a water district board do not meet this definition, the board is not exempt and, to the extent that the board makes decisions on permits, licenses, entitlements for use or engages in other quasi-judicial decision-making, the provisions of Section 84308 are applicable. In the case of hospital districts, it appears that the boards are all directly elected by voters who meet the definition provided in Election Code Section 18. Thus, Section 84308 does not apply.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether Government Code Section 84308(d) (AB 1040) applies to hospital district boards and water district boards, or whether they are exempt as legislative bodies.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Hospital district boards that are directly elected by voters meeting Election Code Section 18's definition are exempt from Section 84308. Water district boards are exempt only if directly elected by such voters; otherwise they must comply with Section 84308 when making quasi-judicial decisions on permits, licenses, and entitlements.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both incoming request letter and FPPC response letter. Extraction based on formal FPPC response dated February 23, 1983. Minor formatting degradation in source document did not impede extraction.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Your question is whether the provisions of this section apply to the members of hospital district boards or water district boards.

CONCLUSION: In the case of water districts, if a board is directly elected by "voters" who meet the definition provided in Election Code Section 18, the board is exempt from the coverage of Section 84308. However, if the voters electing a water district board do not meet this definition, the board is not exempt and, to the extent that the board makes decisions on permits, licenses, entitlements for use or engages in other quasi-judicial decision-making, the provisions of Section 84308 are applicable. In the case of hospital districts, it appears that the boards are all directly elected by voters who meet the definition provided in Election Code Section 18. Thus, Section 84308 does not apply.

embedding.summary: FPPC advises that hospital district boards are exempt from AB 1040 coverage as legislative bodies, while water district boards are exempt only if directly elected by voters meeting Election Code Section 18's definition; non-qualifying water boards must comply with Section 84308.
